items.briefing:
Australians are being warned about a surge in scams impersonating the Australian Taxation Office and myGov. Scammers are using fake messages to trick people into handing over personal or financial information — particularly relevant as end-of-financial-year activity continues. Separately, the Australian Communications and Media Authority (ACMA) has flagged its 2026–27 compliance priorities, with a strong focus on protecting consumers from harmful online activity.

For organisations in the training sector, a notable trend shows cybercriminals are increasingly targeting education software suppliers rather than institutions directly — meaning a breach at a third-party provider could still put your data at risk. **Practical tip:** If you receive any message claiming to be from the ATO or myGov, go directly to the official website rather than clicking any links.

featured.title: ACMA’s 2026–27 compliance priorities focus on protecting consumers
featured.quote: ACMA's compliance priorities directly impact how Australian organizations must manage cybersecurity and consumer protection obligations.
